description: Lock the factory_system node to Arkwright-style water-powered cotton mills beginning at Cromford in 1771, not generic workshops or unsupported business context.

checks.reason: This node uses a decade bucket for Arkwright's late-1760s water-frame patenting and the 1771 Cromford mill, while the scope note names Cromford 1771 explicitly.
Steam power later scaled and relocated textile mills; it did not create the original water-powered Cromford factory system.
The factory system emerged from mechanized textile production after home-based textile work and hand methods.
The scoped factory system was established in water-powered cotton mills.
The scoped factories organized machine tending and long shifts inside large mills.
Banking and investment context may support industrial capitalism, but the inspected factory-system sources do not support it as a direct technology prerequisite.
Clock-time discipline is relevant to later industrial labor systems but is not sourced as a direct prerequisite for the Cromford factory-system scope.
